seoTitle: Hopefield vs Horningsea Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hopefield and Horningsea Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hopefield vs Horningsea Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hopefield and Horningsea Park.
heading: Hopefield vs Horningsea Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hopefield, New South Wales and Horningsea Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Horningsea Park has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Hopefield (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hopefield or Horningsea Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hopefield has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Horningsea Park scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hopefield scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Horningsea Park scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hopefield or Horningsea Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Horningsea Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
